response.justification: While this candidate is an accomplished engineer with 19 years of experience and strong fundamentals in OOP, design patterns, and quality-focused development, they are fundamentally misaligned with this C++-centric industrial automation role. C++ experience ended 11 years ago (2014-2015), with the past decade focused entirely on TypeScript/Node.js web development. Critical domain knowledge is absent: no message brokers, MQTT, industrial protocols (Modbus, IEC 61850), IoT experience, or secure development lifecycle / IEC 62443 background. The candidate would require 6-12 months of foundational ramp-up in both C++ and industrial automation—incompatible with a mid-senior hire expected to contribute immediately.
strengths: Demonstrates strong OOP principles, design patterns, and architectural thinking across 19 years of full-stack development
Proven quality mindset and code craftsmanship: TDD practitioner, active in testing and mentoring, organized technical dojos
Shows high ownership and problem-solving across diverse domains (geospatial, high-frequency trading, direct mail automation)
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (6 months) and ended 11 years ago (2014-2015); past decade entirely TypeScript/Node.js web-focused—proficiency decay is significant risk
No exposure to industrial automation, IoT, message brokers, MQTT, or industrial protocols (Modbus, IEC 61850, OPC UA)—domain knowledge is entirely absent
No evidence of secure development lifecycle, IEC 62443 compliance, or industrial cybersecurity practices—a core responsibility of this role

key_factors.factor: Industrial automation and IoT domain experience
